briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To extract rich metadata from DOCX documents — reads core properties (author, dates, keywords, etc.) via python-docx and merges them with base OS metadata.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: DOCX file metadata: core properties, subject, keywords, author, dates. \par \b Input\/ \: file_path via extract(), config with recognized formats. \par \b Output\/ \: dict merging BaseMetadataExtractor fields + DOCX-specific core properties (document_subject, keywords, category, comments, author, last_modified_by, created_date, modified_date, last_printed_date). \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): BaseMetadataExtractor, python-docx Document.core_properties] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: extract() ALWAYS returns a dict with base metadata merged.
On PackageNotFoundError, returns {"broken_docx": True} instead of raising. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why use BaseMetadataExtractor as a delegate instead of inheritance? A: Composition allows combining base file metadata with format-specific properties without coupling the DOCX extractor to os.stat internals. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 — Semantic template markup and LDD logging added.] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 8[DOCX metadata extractor with core properties] => DocxMetadataExtractor \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
